latest.title: Form 8.3 - IQE Plc
latest.content:
Today 15:00
RNS Number : 8164Z
Richard Griffiths
09 April 2026
PUBLIC DEALING DISCLOSURE BY PERSONS WITH INTERESTS IN RELEVANT SECURITIES REPRESENTING 1% OR MORE
Rule 8.3 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")
1. KEY INFORMATION
(a) Full name of discloser:
Richard Griffiths
(b) Owner or controller of interests and short positions disclosed, if different from 1(a):
The naming of nominee or vehicle companies is insufficient. For a trust, the trustee(s), settlor and beneficiaries must be named.
N/A
(c) Name of offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:
Use a separate form for each offeree
IQE Plc
(d) If an exempt fund manager connected with an offeree, state this and specify identity of offeree:
N/A
(e) Date position held/dealing undertaken:
For an opening position disclosure, state the latest practicable date prior to the disclosure
8 April 2026
(f) In addition to the company in 1(c) above, is the discloser making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?
If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"
No
2. POSITIONS OF THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE
If there are positions or rights to subscribe to disclose in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 2(a) or (b) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security.
(a) Interests and short positions in the relevant securities of the offeror or offeree to which the disclosure relates following the dealing (if any)
Class of relevant security:
Ordinary GBP
Interests
Short positions
Number
%
Number
%
(1) Relevant securities owned and/or controlled:
56,348,639
5.75%
(2) Cash-settled derivatives:
28,011,944
2.86%
(3) Stock-settled derivatives (including options) and agreements to purchase/sell:
TOTAL:
84,360,583
8.61%
All interests and all short positions should be disclosed.
Details of any open stock-settled derivative positions (including traded options), or agreements to purchase or sell relevant securities, should be given on a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions).
(b) Rights to subscribe for new securities (including directors' and other employee options)
Class of relevant security in relation to which subscription right exists:
N/A
Details, including nature of the rights concerned and relevant percentages:
3. DEALINGS (IF ANY) BY THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE
Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 3(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.
The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.
(a) Purchases and sales
Class of relevant security
Purchase/sale
Number of securities
Price per unit
(b) Cash-settled derivative transactions
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. CFD
Nature of dealing
e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position
Number of reference securities
Price per unit
Ordinary
CFD
Close long position
1,483,174
28.219 pence
(c) Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)
(i) Writing, selling, purchasing or varying
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.
Number of securities to which option relates
Exercise price per unit

e.g. American, European etc.

(ii) Exercise

(d) Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)
Class of relevant security

e.g. subscription, conversion
Details
Price per unit (if applicable)
4. OTHER INFORMATION
(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements
Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the person making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:
Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives
Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the person making the disclosure and any other person relating to:
(i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or
(ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:
If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(c) Attachments
Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?
No
Date of disclosure:
9 April 2026
Contact name:
James Sutcliffe
Telephone number:
Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service and must also be emailed to the Takeover Panel at
monitoring@disclosure.org.uk
. The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.
The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at
www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk
.

company_30s.what_they_do: IQE plc is a specialist semiconductor manufacturer that produces high‑performance silicon wafers used by chip makers for logic, RF and power devices. The company also offers wafer‑level testing and packaging support to its customers.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Silicon wafers are the backbone of every advanced chip. IQE’s wafers are prized for their low defect rates and high purity, making them a go‑to supplier for leading AI, automotive and telecom chip designers. With a long‑term backlog that includes contracts with major fab‑less and foundry players, the company sits at a critical junction in the global semiconductor supply chain.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is emerging from a severe liquidity crunch that forced a senior‑management overhaul on March 30th. It is tightening costs and focusing on its core wafer business. While the share price is trading at a steep discount, the company still enjoys a sizable order backlog and a clear plan to rebuild cash flow.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show a complete replacement of senior executives and a new compliance framework aimed at strengthening governance. This signals a fresh start and a commitment to operational discipline.

The company is also tightening its focus on the wafer segment, trimming non‑core assets and pledging to invest in process upgrades that will keep its products competitive for AI and automotive markets.
These moves suggest the company is stabilising and positioning itself to capture the next wave of chip demand, especially as AI and 5G drive silicon consumption.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the market is largely silent, but a handful of bullish voices highlight AI and semiconductor growth.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Investors are pointing to the company’s role in AI chip production and the long‑term demand for high‑purity wafers. They see the management change as a clean break from past missteps.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some commentators worry that the photonics sector is still weak and that regulatory scrutiny could hit the industry. Others fear that the company’s cost base remains too high to sustain profitability.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from cautious to cautiously bullish as AI buzz grows, but overall chatter remains low, leaving room for surprise.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for IQE is flat – there is no current public interest, which is a green light for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming earnings release could reveal stronger-than‑expected order flow and a clearer cash‑flow outlook.
A potential partnership announcement with a major AI chip designer would validate the demand narrative.
A volume spike at the 12p support level could signal that capitulation is finished and a bounce is beginning.
Positive commentary from industry analysts about the company’s wafer upgrades would boost confidence.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained rise in shares traded over 50 k per day, a break above the 20p resistance, and any forward guidance that lifts the order backlog.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is still in the early recovery phase of a deep crash. Confirmation of mean reversion will likely take 3–6 months. A breakout above 12p with volume will be the first clear sign that the market is moving in the contrarian direction.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen 70–95 % from its peak, creating a huge upside potential. If it rebounds to the 30p level that is frequently mentioned in bullish posts, the return would be roughly 200 % from the current price. Institutional accumulation signals are likely to follow a deep bottom, and the company’s core wafer business is positioned for AI‑driven growth.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are continued weak demand in the photonics space, regulatory headwinds, and the possibility that the cost‑cutting plan may not materialise quickly enough. A worst‑case scenario would see the share stay below 5p, eroding any upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current price; upside is about 200 % if the share hits 30p. The asymmetry is roughly 1:2, making the trade attractive for a contrarian stance.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above 12p with volume over 50 k shares. Allocate 10 % of the portfolio to the position and place a stop 5p below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the share touches 15p with a volume spike and the earnings call confirms a positive outlook. Only then consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: No immediate terminal risk identified; therefore, the play remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash, a quiet but growing AI narrative, and a clear path to 30p give this contrarian play a 200 % upside potential with a limited downside.
